question: [File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: IRatifier.isRatified / EcrecoverRatifier.isRatified] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that a market is created with MAX_COLLATERALS (128) collateral entries in market.collateralParams, trigger take(offer, ratifierData, ...) where offer.market.collateralParams.length == 128, causing EcrecoverRatifier.isRatified to invoke HashLib.hashOffer -> HashLib.hashMarket which loops 128 times over collateralParamsHashes, violating the invariant that a single take() call must not consume unbounded gas proportional to attacker-controlled collateral list length, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS of any taker or maker whose offer references a max-collateral market by making take() prohibitively expensive? Proof idea: fuzz market.collateralParams.length from 1 to 128 and measure gas consumed by isRatified; assert gas grows linearly and crosses block gas limit at some threshold.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: SetterRatifier.isRatified / HashLib.hashOffer] Can an unprivileged market creator, under the precondition that they create a market with 128 collateral params and publish a SetterRatifier-backed offer referencing that market, trigger take() causing SetterRatifier.isRatified to call HashLib.hashOffer -> HashLib.hashMarket with a 128-element loop, violating the invariant that ratifier gas cost must be bounded regardless of market configuration, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where legitimate takers cannot economically execute against offers in max-collateral markets? Proof idea: deploy SetterRatifier, create 128-collateral market, measure gas of isRatified call with proof.length == 0 vs proof.length == 20.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: EcrecoverRatifier.isRatified / HashLib.isLeaf] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that ratifierData encodes a Merkle proof of maximum height (proof.length == 20), trigger take() causing HashLib.isLeaf to iterate 20 times with keccak256 per iteration, violating the invariant that ratifier verification cost must not be attacker-scalable via ratifierData size, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where the taker pays disproportionate gas for proof verification even when the offer is valid? Proof idea: fuzz proof.length from 0 to 20 and assert gas cost of isRatified scales linearly; check if a malicious taker can force a maker's callback to OOG by composing max-proof takes in multicall.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: HashLib.hashOffer / offer.callbackData] Can an unprivileged maker, under the precondition that they embed arbitrarily large callbackData in an Offer struct (callbackData is bytes with no length cap), trigger take() causing HashLib.hashOffer to call keccak256(offer.callbackData) with O(n) gas cost proportional to callbackData.length, violating the invariant that offer hashing cost must be bounded, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where any taker who executes against an offer with large callbackData pays unbounded hashing cost inside isRatified? Proof idea: fuzz offer.callbackData.length from 0 to 100_000 bytes and measure gas consumed by EcrecoverRatifier.isRatified; assert cost grows linearly.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.touchMarket / market creation loop] Can an unprivileged market creator, under the precondition that they call touchMarket with market.collateralParams.length == 128, trigger the collateral validation loop at Midnight.sol:762 to iterate 128 times (address comparison, isLltvAllowed, maxLif check per iteration), violating the invariant that market creation gas cost must not be attacker-scalable to block subsequent market interactions, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where creating a max-collateral market makes all subsequent take/liquidate/isHealthy calls on that market expensive? Proof idea: fuzz collateralParams.length from 1 to 128 and measure touchMarket gas; assert it does not approach block gas limit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.isHealthy / collateralBitmap while-loop] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that they activate MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER (16) collaterals by calling supplyCollateral 16 times across distinct collateral indices, trigger isHealthy to iterate 16 times calling IOracle.price() per iteration, violating the invariant that health check gas cost must be bounded by MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER and not cause OOG in downstream callers (take, withdrawCollateral), causing scoped impact: fund freeze where a borrower with 16 activated collaterals cannot be health-checked within a reasonable gas budget, blocking withdrawCollateral and take for that seller? Proof idea: activate 16 collaterals, call isHealthy and measure gas; assert it stays well below 1M gas even with 16 oracle calls.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / collateralBitmap while-loop] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that a borrower has activated 16 collaterals (collateralBitmap has 16 bits set), trigger liquidate() to iterate the while(_collateralBitmap != 0) loop 16 times calling IOracle(_collateralParam.oracle).price() per iteration, violating the invariant that liquidation must remain executable within block gas limits regardless of borrower collateral count, causing scoped impact: liquidation failure where unhealthy positions with 16 activated collaterals cannot be liquidated due to gas exhaustion? Proof idea: create borrower with 16 activated collaterals, attempt liquidate() and measure gas; assert liquidation succeeds within 3M gas.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.multicall / delegatecall loop] Can an unprivileged user, under the precondition that they compose a multicall with a large calls[] array (no length cap enforced), trigger multicall to iterate calls.length times with delegatecall per iteration, violating the invariant that multicall must not allow a single transaction to consume unbounded gas or bypass per-call invariants through accumulated state changes, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a user submits a multicall with thousands of no-op calls to grief block space or cause OOG for other users in the same block? Proof idea: fuzz calls.length from 1 to 10_000 with no-op payloads and measure gas; assert multicall reverts or is bounded before block gas limit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + take composition] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that they compose a multicall with N take() calls each referencing a 128-collateral market with a 20-level Merkle proof, trigger multiplicative gas consumption (N * 128 * 20 keccak256 operations) in a single transaction, violating the invariant that multicall gas cost must not be superlinearly amplified by collateral list size times proof depth, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a single multicall transaction consumes the entire block gas limit, preventing other users from transacting? Proof idea: measure gas of multicall([take_128_collateral_20_proof] * N) and find the minimum N that exhausts block gas.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.flashLoan / token loop] Can an unprivileged caller, under the precondition that they pass a tokens[] array of length L (no cap enforced) to flashLoan(), trigger two sequential loops each iterating L times (safeTransfer then safeTransferFrom), violating the invariant that flashLoan gas cost must be bounded by a reasonable token count, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a flashLoan with a large tokens array exhausts block gas and prevents other protocol interactions in the same block? Proof idea: fuzz tokens.length from 1 to 1000 and measure flashLoan gas; assert it does not approach block gas limit at reasonable token counts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: EcrecoverRatifier.isRatified / HashLib.hashMarket collateral loop] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that a valid offer references a market with 128 collaterals and the taker passes ratifierData with a 20-level proof, trigger isRatified to perform 128 hashCollateralParams calls plus 20 hashNode calls in a single view call, violating the invariant that the ratifier's view function must not be a gas sink that makes take() economically unviable, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where offers in large-collateral markets become permanently untakeable due to gas cost exceeding taker willingness to pay? Proof idea: unit test isRatified with collateralParams.length == 128 and proof.length == 20; assert total gas < 500_000.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.take / isRatified + isHealthy sequential loops] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that the seller has 16 activated collaterals and the offer references a 128-collateral market with a 20-level proof, trigger take() to sequentially execute isRatified (128-collateral hash loop + 20-node proof loop) followed by isHealthy (16-oracle loop), violating the invariant that a single take() must complete within a reasonable gas budget regardless of collateral list sizes, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where take() for a seller with many collaterals in a large-collateral market always OOGs? Proof idea: compose worst-case take() with 128-collateral market, 20-level proof, seller with 16 activated collaterals; measure total gas.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / takes loop + collateralWithdrawals loop] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that they pass a takes[] array of length T and a collateralWithdrawals[] array of length W (no caps enforced in MidnightBundles), trigger the bundler to iterate T take() calls followed by W withdrawCollateral() calls, violating the invariant that bundler gas cost must be bounded by reasonable array sizes, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a taker submits a bundle with large arrays to exhaust block gas and grief other users? Proof idea: fuzz takes.length and collateralWithdrawals.length independently from 1 to 500; measure total gas and assert it stays below block gas limit at expected usage sizes.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget / collateralSupplies loop] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that they pass a collateralSupplies[] array of length S with distinct collateral indices, trigger the bundler to iterate S times calling supplyCollateral() per iteration (each touching storage and potentially calling safeTransferFrom), violating the invariant that collateral supply batching must not allow unbounded gas consumption in a single bundler call, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a large collateralSupplies array exhausts block gas? Proof idea: fuzz collateralSupplies.length from 1 to 128 and measure gas; assert it stays below 5M gas.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.touchMarket / collateralParams validation loop + isRatified hash loop] Can an unprivileged market creator, under the precondition that they create a market with 128 collaterals and immediately take an offer in the same transaction via multicall([touchMarket, take]), trigger touchMarket's 128-iteration validation loop followed by take's isRatified 128-iteration hash loop in a single transaction, violating the invariant that market creation and first take must not together exhaust block gas, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where the first take on a new max-collateral market always fails due to combined gas cost? Proof idea: multicall([touchMarket(128-collateral market), take(offer with 128-collateral market, 20-level proof)]) and measure total gas.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / badDebt loop + repay branch] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that a borrower has 16 activated collaterals all with bad debt (badDebt > 0 for each), trigger liquidate() to iterate the collateralBitmap while-loop 16 times computing maxDebt and badDebt, then execute the lossFactor update and repay branch, violating the invariant that bad debt realization during liquidation must complete within block gas limits for max-collateral borrowers, causing scoped impact: liquidation failure where bad-debt positions with 16 collaterals cannot be liquidated, leaving protocol insolvent? Proof idea: create borrower with 16 collaterals all underwater, call liquidate() and measure gas; assert it completes within 2M gas.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.isHealthy / oracle call loop + take health check] Can an unprivileged seller, under the precondition that they have 16 activated collaterals each backed by a gas-expensive oracle (e.g., a Chainlink aggregator with many rounds), trigger take() to call isHealthy which iterates 16 oracle price() calls, violating the invariant that the health check at the end of take() must not be bypassable by making it too expensive, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where a seller with 16 expensive-oracle collaterals cannot participate in take() because the health check always OOGs? Proof idea: mock 16 oracles each consuming 50_000 gas per price() call; measure isHealthy gas with 16 activated collaterals.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + liquidate composition] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that they compose a multicall with M liquidate() calls each targeting a borrower with 16 activated collaterals, trigger M * 16 oracle calls in a single transaction, violating the invariant that multicall must not amplify per-call oracle costs to exhaust block gas, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a liquidator's multicall of liquidations exhausts block gas and prevents other liquidations from occurring in the same block? Proof idea: measure gas of multicall([liquidate(16-collateral borrower)] * M) and find the minimum M that approaches block gas limit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: HashLib.hashMarket / collateralParamsHashes assembly loop] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that an offer's market has 128 collateral params, trigger HashLib.hashMarket to allocate a bytes32[128] array in memory and iterate 128 times calling hashCollateralParams (each doing abi.encode + keccak256), violating the invariant that memory allocation and hashing in isRatified must not cause memory expansion costs to make take() uneconomical, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where memory expansion for 128-element array in hashMarket adds significant unexpected gas overhead? Proof idea: measure memory gas cost of hashMarket with collateralParams.length == 128 vs 1; assert the delta is bounded and documented.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + isHealthy composition via withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that they have 16 activated collaterals and compose a multicall with N withdrawCollateral() calls each triggering isHealthy, trigger N * 16 oracle calls in a single multicall transaction, violating the invariant that multicall must not allow a single user to amplify oracle call costs across multiple health checks, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a borrower's multicall of withdrawals exhausts block gas? Proof idea: multicall([withdrawCollateral(1 wei)] * N) with 16 activated collaterals; measure gas and find minimum N that approaches block gas limit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: EcrecoverRatifier.isRatified / ecrecover + isAuthorized check] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that the offer's maker has authorized a long chain of delegated signers (isAuthorized[maker][signer] == true), trigger isRatified to call IMidnight(MIDNIGHT).isAuthorized(offer.maker, _signer) as an external call after ecrecover, violating the invariant that the ratifier's external call to Midnight must not be reentrant or gas-manipulable, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where the external isAuthorized call adds unexpected cross-contract call overhead in the hot path of take()? Proof idea: measure gas of isRatified with _signer == offer.maker (no external call) vs _signer != offer.maker (external isAuthorized call); assert delta is bounded.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.take / _updatePosition + isHealthy sequential oracle loops] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that both buyer and seller have credit (triggering _updatePosition for both) and the seller has 16 activated collaterals, trigger take() to call _updatePosition twice followed by isHealthy with 16 oracle calls, violating the invariant that the combined gas cost of position updates and health check must not make take() uneconomical for max-collateral sellers, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where take() against a seller with 16 collaterals always exceeds reasonable gas limits? Proof idea: measure take() gas with seller having 16 activated collaterals and both buyer/seller having credit; assert total gas < 2M.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.touchMarket / collateralParams loop called from take] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that they call take() on a market that has not yet been touched (tickSpacing == 0), trigger touchMarket inside take() to iterate the 128-collateral validation loop before proceeding with the rest of take() logic, violating the invariant that the first take on a new market must not be significantly more expensive than subsequent takes, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where the first taker on a max-collateral market pays disproportionate gas for market initialization? Proof idea: compare gas of take() on an already-touched vs not-yet-touched 128-collateral market; assert the delta is bounded and expected.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / try-catch take loop] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that they pass a takes[] array where all offers revert (e.g., all expired), trigger the bundler's for loop to iterate takes.length times each executing a try-catch take() call that always reverts, violating the invariant that the bundler must not allow an attacker to force O(N) failed external calls to grief gas, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a taker submits a bundle with N always-reverting offers to waste block gas? Proof idea: pass takes[] with N expired offers; measure gas of buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral and assert it reverts with OutOfOffers() after bounded gas.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / collateralBitmap loop with reverting oracle] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that they activate 16 collaterals where one oracle reverts on price(), trigger liquidate() to iterate the collateralBitmap while-loop until it hits the reverting oracle, causing liquidate() to revert after consuming gas for all preceding oracle calls, violating the invariant that a borrower must not be able to make their position permanently unliquidatable by activating a collateral with a reverting oracle, causing scoped impact: liquidation failure where an unhealthy borrower is permanently protected from liquidation? Proof idea: activate 16 collaterals with the last one having a reverting oracle; call liquidate() and assert it reverts; verify the borrower cannot be liquidated.
